title: Transformers, the tech behind LLMs
description: A visual walkthrough of how transformers turn text into predictions, from token embeddings through attention and MLP blocks to the final unembedding.

scene.default_input: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy

narration.text: This is a transformer, the architecture behind large language models like GPT. At its heart, it is a machine that takes in text and predicts what word comes next.

args.text: A transformer: text in, next-word prediction out

narration.text: First the input text is broken into tokens, little chunks of words. Each token becomes a high-dimensional vector, an embedding that encodes its meaning as a point in space.
These vectors flow forward through a stack of blocks. As they pass through, each vector gets nudged and enriched, soaking up context from its neighbors.
The defining piece is attention. Attention lets every token look at every other token and pull in the information it needs to refine its meaning.

args.text: Each token attends to the others

narration.text: Attention runs in parallel across many heads. Each head learns its own kind of relationship, like matching a word to its subject or tracking the topic.
A different head focuses on a completely different pattern. Together the heads build a rich, multi-faceted picture of the context.
After attention, each vector passes through a multi-layer perceptron. This is where much of the model's stored knowledge lives, applied to each token independently.
This pattern, attention then MLP, repeats block after block. With each layer the representations grow more abstract and more capable.
Look at attention deeper in the network. By now the heads can attend across long ranges, capturing meaning that spans the whole sentence.
At the very end, the final token's vector is projected back out to a score for every possible next token. That is the unembedding step.

args.text: Vector to scores over the vocabulary

narration.text: A softmax turns those scores into probabilities. The model picks a likely next token, appends it, and repeats the whole process to generate text.
Let's feed the model a real sentence and watch it light up. Given this prompt, the activations flow and the model predicts the next word.

args.text: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy
Forward pass on a real prompt

narration.text: Here are the actual activations at an early block during that run, the numbers the model is really computing as it reads the prompt.
So a transformer is just embeddings, attention, and MLPs repeated many times, ending in a prediction. Scale this up with billions of parameters and you get the language models reshaping our world.

affordances.description: Move the camera to frame a part of the model.

args.target: one of: overview, embeddings, block, attention, mlp, head, unembed, token
args.index: block index (0-based) when target is block/attention/mlp/head
args.head: attention head index when target is head
args.tokenIndex: token index when target is token

affordances.description: Highlight a transformer block by index.
Highlight and focus a specific attention head in a block.
Draw the attention pattern (token-to-token arcs) for a block. Omit head to average over all heads.
Show residual-stream activations at a block as a heatmap; mode 'values' also prints numbers.
Set decimal places shown for numeric values.
Run the real model forward pass on input text and light up activations/attention.
